title: senecio sericeus var. candolleanus
intro: Senecio sericeus var. candolleanus, also known as Candolle's woolly groundsel, is a variety of flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family and is native to the mountainous regions of South Africa. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a diaphoretic, helping to induce sweating, and as an expectorant, aiding in the removal of mucus from the lungs. The health benefits of Senecio sericeus var. candolleanus include its potential to help reduce fever, relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, and even exhibit anti-inflammatory properties. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
